denial_letter_text:
AETNA

Date of Notice: October 5, 2026

Dear Patient,

We have completed our review of the prior authorization request submitted by your provider on September 1, 2026, for Inpatient myomectomy (Diagnosis: Symptomatic uterine fibroids, D25.9).

Based on Aetna medical policy, your request is denied because Medical Necessity criteria have not been met. Our review indicates that a lower level of care is appropriate for this condition. Inpatient admission for a myomectomy exceeds what is required, as outpatient management has not been exhausted per clinical guidelines. 

Additionally, the requested surgical approach is considered experimental or investigational for your condition. Finally, per Aetna pediatric fibroid guideline CPB-123 (applicable to patients under age 18), the clinical criteria for inpatient admission are not satisfied.

Therefore, the request for inpatient admission is not covered.

You or your provider may appeal this decision within 180 days.

Additional plan language regarding experimental/investigational definitions, network status, and member cost-sharing may apply and is available upon request.
clinical_context: The patient is a 45-year-old female with symptomatic uterine fibroids (D25.9) presenting with heavy menstrual bleeding and iron deficiency anemia (Hgb 9.8). She has failed hormonal management and desires fertility preservation. Her gynecologic surgeon recommended an inpatient myomectomy due to the size and location of the fibroids, which carry a high risk of intraoperative bleeding. A comprehensive clinical package and letter of medical necessity were submitted requesting prior authorization for inpatient admission.
